title: Tool Agent Eval Scenarios
description: Deterministic behavioral scenarios for the tool agent eval runner.

$schema.description: Relative or absolute path to this JSON schema for editor integration.

scenarios.description: Deterministic scenarios executed by the eval runner.

workspace.description: Files and metadata used to recreate the live eval working directory.

reference_time.description: Optional ISO datetime used by relative workspace dates. Defaults to the local time when the workspace is prepared.

files.description: Workspace files to create before live evals run.

path.description: Workspace-relative file path. Absolute paths and parent-directory traversal are rejected.

content.description: UTF-8 text content written to the file. Defaults to an empty string.

size_bytes.description: Optional exact UTF-8 byte size. Content is padded with # characters when needed.

relative_datetime.description: Datetime resolved relative to the workspace reference_time.

time.description: ISO time used with the resolved date.

id.description: Stable scenario identifier used in reports.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the behavior under test.

turns.description: Conversation turns executed sequentially against one agent instance.

fixtures.description: Deterministic tool return values used by the scripted test registry.

directory_listing.description: Optional legacy override for list_current_directory. Prefer top-level workspace files so deterministic and live evals share one source of truth.

input.description: User message sent to the agent.

llm_responses.description: Scripted assistant messages returned by the fake LLM for this turn.

expected_tools.description: Exact ordered list of tool names expected for this turn.

forbidden_tools.description: Tool names that must not be used on this turn.

content.description: Assistant text returned by the scripted LLM.

reasoning_content.description: Optional reasoning text captured in traces for debugging.

tool_calls.description: Native-looking tool calls returned by the scripted LLM.

id.description: Tool call id. If omitted, the fixture layer creates one.

arguments.description: JSON arguments sent to the tool.

answer_contains.description: Case-insensitive text fragments that must appear in the final answer.

answer_not_contains.description: Case-insensitive text fragments that must not appear in the final answer.

memory_valid.description: Validate assistant tool calls are followed by matching tool messages.

grounded_in_tool_results.description: Validate obvious file names, byte sizes, and access claims against tool results.

date_answer_matches_tool_result.description: Validate relative date answers against get_current_time output.

fallback_used.description: Assert whether a deterministic fallback was used for the final answer.
